# Week 29 Family Cookbook Packet

Week of: Sunday, July 12, 2026 through Saturday, July 18, 2026  
Packet owner: Family  
Planning status: Draft  

## Week Goals

- Keep meals protein-forward.
- Preserve international variety across the week.
- Schedule perishable ingredients first.
- Use clear grocery specifications.
- Use arrowroot instead of cornstarch unless a recipe specifically requires cornstarch.
- Test Stage 1 recipes before promoting anything to Stage 2.

## Weekly Menu

| Day | Meal | Recipe file | Stage | Protein | Cuisine/flavor | Perishability reason | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sunday, July 12 | Peruvian Chicken with Green Sauce, Roasted Potatoes, and Simple Salad | `peruvian-chicken-green-sauce-roasted-potatoes-simple-salad.md` | Stage 1 | Chicken | Peruvian-inspired pollo a la brasa | Uses fresh chicken, cilantro, jalapeno, lettuce, and avocado oil sauce ingredients | Signature Sunday grill meal |
| Monday, July 13 | Orange Beef with Jasmine Rice and Broccoli | `orange-beef-jasmine-rice-broccoli.md` | Stage 1 | Beef | Chinese-American citrus takeout-inspired | Fresh beef, oranges, and broccoli are best early | Quick weeknight meal |
| Tuesday, July 14 | Chicken Cordon Bleu Skillet with Mashed Potatoes | `chicken-cordon-bleu-skillet-mashed-potatoes.md` | Stage 1 | Chicken breast | French-inspired skillet comfort food | Chicken, ham, Swiss, cream, and potatoes should cook early-midweek | No breading, creamy skillet version |
| Wednesday, July 15 | Vietnamese Lemongrass Pork Bowls with Pickled Vegetables and Herbs | `vietnamese-lemongrass-pork-bowls-pickled-vegetables.md` | Stage 1 | Pork chops | Vietnamese-inspired lemongrass, fish sauce, herbs | Pork chops and fresh herbs are best midweek | Bright rice bowl |
| Thursday, July 16 | Mediterranean Turkey Meatballs with Lemon Rice, Tzatziki, and Roasted Vegetables | `mediterranean-turkey-meatballs-lemon-rice-tzatziki-roasted-vegetables.md` | Stage 1 | Ground turkey | Greek/Mediterranean | Ground turkey, yogurt, cucumbers, and vegetables need freshness | Test turkey meatball texture |
| Friday, July 17 | Chimichurri Steak with Roasted Potatoes and Simple Salad | `chimichurri-steak-roasted-potatoes-simple-salad.md` | Stage 1 | Steak | Argentinian-inspired grilled steak | Steak and parsley-cilantro chimichurri are best before weekend | Signature grill meal |
| Saturday, July 18 | BBQ Chicken Flatbreads | `bbq-chicken-flatbreads.md` | Stage 1 | Chicken | American BBQ | Flatbreads, cheese, and cooked chicken hold well for late week | Quick, fun, good leftovers |

## Recipes This Week

New recipes should start as Stage 1 drafts in this weekly folder. After cooking and review, move approved recipes to the archive with family ratings, version notes, and any changes.

| Mediterranean Turkey Meatballs with Lemon Rice, Tzatziki, and Roasted Vegetables | `mediterranean-turkey-meatballs-lemon-rice-tzatziki-roasted-vegetables.md` | Stage 1 | Test turkey meatball moisture and bowl components | Yes |
| Chimichurri Steak with Roasted Potatoes and Simple Salad | `chimichurri-steak-roasted-potatoes-simple-salad.md` | Stage 1 | Test steak doneness and chimichurri brightness | Yes |
| BBQ Chicken Flatbreads | `bbq-chicken-flatbreads.md` | Stage 1 | Test crisp flatbread texture and leftover quality | Yes |

## Grocery List

Rules:

- Recipe-driven only.

## Prep Guide

Every prep task should include ingredients, instructions, storage method, use-by date, and meal ownership.

### Sunday Dinner Support

- [ ] Make Peruvian chicken marinade.
  - Ingredients: Garlic, lime juice, neutral oil, cumin, smoked paprika, oregano, salt, pepper.
  - Instructions: Mix marinade, coat chicken thoroughly, and refrigerate.
  - Storage method: Covered container or zip-top bag in refrigerator.
  - Use-by date: Cook Sunday, July 12.
  - Meal ownership: Peruvian Chicken.
- [ ] Make green sauce.
  - Ingredients: Cilantro, jalapeno, garlic, lime juice, Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, salt.
  - Instructions: Blend until smooth. Taste for salt and heat.
  - Storage method: Covered jar in refrigerator.
  - Use-by date: Sunday, July 12, or Monday for leftovers.
  - Meal ownership: Peruvian Chicken.

### Future Meal Prep

- [ ] Slice orange beef and cut broccoli.
  - Ingredients: Flank steak, broccoli.
  - Instructions: Slice beef thinly against the grain and cut broccoli into florets.
  - Storage method: Separate covered containers in refrigerator.
  - Use-by date: Monday, July 13.
  - Meal ownership: Orange Beef.
- [ ] Prep cordon bleu skillet components.
  - Ingredients: Chicken breast, ham, Swiss, potatoes.
  - Instructions: Cut chicken into even cutlets or chunks, slice ham and cheese, and peel potatoes if desired.
  - Storage method: Separate covered containers in refrigerator.
  - Use-by date: Tuesday, July 14.
  - Meal ownership: Chicken Cordon Bleu Skillet.
- [ ] Make Vietnamese pickled vegetables.
  - Ingredients: Carrots, cucumber, radishes, rice vinegar, sugar, salt.
  - Instructions: Slice vegetables thinly, stir with quick pickle brine, and refrigerate.
  - Storage method: Covered jar or container in refrigerator.
  - Use-by date: Wednesday, July 15.
  - Meal ownership: Lemongrass Pork Bowls.

### Protein Thaw Schedule

| Protein | Recipe | Move from freezer to fridge | Cook date | Backup plan |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Chicken leg quarters or thighs, 6 lb | Peruvian Chicken | Friday, July 10 | Sunday, July 12 | Buy fresh chicken Sunday morning |
| Flank steak, 1 1/2 lb | Orange Beef | Saturday, July 11 | Monday, July 13 | Use sirloin strips |
| Chicken breasts, 2 lb | Cordon Bleu Skillet | Sunday, July 12 | Tuesday, July 14 | Buy fresh chicken breasts |
| Pork chops, 1 1/2 lb | Lemongrass Pork Bowls | Monday, July 13 | Wednesday, July 15 | Use pork tenderloin |
| Ground turkey, 1 1/2 lb | Turkey Meatballs | Tuesday, July 14 | Thursday, July 16 | Buy fresh ground turkey |
| Flank or skirt steak, 2 lb | Chimichurri Steak | Wednesday, July 15 | Friday, July 17 | Use sirloin steak |
| Chicken thighs, 2 lb | BBQ Chicken Flatbreads | Thursday, July 16 | Saturday, July 18 | Use rotisserie chicken if needed |

### Wednesday Refresh

- [ ] Check herbs and salad produce.
  - Ingredients: Cilantro, parsley, mint, basil, romaine, tomatoes, cucumbers.
  - Instructions: Discard spoiled leaves, dry damp herbs, and move Thursday-Friday produce to the front of the fridge.
  - Storage method: Paper-towel-lined containers or produce drawers.
  - Use-by date: Thursday, July 16 and Friday, July 17.
  - Meal ownership: Turkey Meatballs, Chimichurri Steak.
- [ ] Make tzatziki.
  - Ingredients: Greek yogurt, cucumber, lemon, garlic, dill, salt, pepper.
  - Instructions: Grate and squeeze cucumber dry, then mix sauce.
  - Storage method: Covered container in refrigerator.
  - Use-by date: Thursday, July 16.
  - Meal ownership: Mediterranean Turkey Meatballs.

### Do Not Prep Ahead

| Item | Recipe | Reason not to prep early | Prep timing |
|---|---|---|---|
| Simple salad | Peruvian Chicken, Chimichurri Steak | Lettuce wilts once dressed | Wash and dress day-of |
| Orange beef sauce with arrowroot | Orange Beef | Thickener can get gummy if held too long | Mix sauce Monday |
| Mashed potatoes | Cordon Bleu Skillet | Best texture fresh | Cook Tuesday |
| Fresh herbs for bowls | Lemongrass Pork | Herbs bruise and darken | Wash and chop Wednesday |
| Roasted vegetables | Turkey Meatballs | Best browned and hot | Roast Thursday |
| Flatbread assembly | BBQ Chicken Flatbreads | Bread can get soggy | Assemble and bake Saturday |

## Reviews Needed After Cooking

Use `templates/recipe-review.md` for each Stage 1 recipe tested this week.

Only list recipes here after the family has cooked them and decided they are keepers.

## Leftover Plan

- Planned leftovers: BBQ chicken flatbreads should reheat well in the oven or air fryer. Orange beef, lemongrass pork, and turkey meatballs can become rice bowls.
- Lunch conversions: Peruvian chicken can become salad bowls with green sauce. Chimichurri steak can become steak salad or rice bowls.
- Freeze portions: Turkey meatballs can freeze after cooking if cooled completely.
- Do not save: Dressed salads and herb toppings should be kept separate and used quickly.

## Lessons Learned This Week

Add useful takeaways to `planning/lessons-learned.md` after the week is complete.

- 

## Open Questions For Next Week

- Was the Peruvian green sauce keeper-level enough to archive on its own?
- Did two steak/beef meals feel too heavy, or did the flavor variety make it work?
- Was the flatbread night easy enough for a regular Saturday rotation?

recipes.title: BBQ Chicken Flatbreads
recipes.markdown:
# BBQ Chicken Flatbreads

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-29/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 15 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 25 minutes
- Protein: Chicken thighs
- Cuisine or flavor direction: American BBQ, smoky, cheesy, tangy
- Best day to cook: Saturday
- Perishability notes: Chicken, cheese, and flatbreads hold well for late week if kept cold.
- Difficulty: Easy

## Equipment

- Sheet pan
- Skillet
- Cutting board and knife
- Mixing bowl
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
| ---: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 lb | Boneless skinless chicken thighs | Trimmed | Chicken breast, watched carefully | Main protein |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Chicken |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Chicken |
| 1 tsp | Smoked paprika | Smoked | Sweet paprika | Chicken |
| 1/2 tsp | Garlic powder | Ground | 2 cloves fresh garlic | Chicken |
| 1/2 tsp | Onion powder | Ground | Skip if unavailable | Chicken |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Chicken |
| 1 cup | BBQ sauce | Favorite smoky-sweet sauce | Homemade BBQ sauce | Sauce |
| 4 large | Flatbreads or naan | Sturdy | Pizza crusts or pita | Base |
| 1 cup | Mozzarella cheese | Shredded low-moisture | Italian cheese blend | Topping |
| 1 cup | Cheddar cheese | Shredded | Monterey Jack | Topping |
| 1 | Red onion | Thinly sliced | Shallot | Topping |
| 1 | Red bell pepper | Thinly sliced | Orange bell pepper | Topping |
| 1 cup | Red cabbage | Thinly sliced | Bagged coleslaw mix | Fresh finish |
| 1 tbsp | Apple cider vinegar | Apple cider vinegar | White vinegar | Cabbage |
| 1 tsp | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Honey | Cabbage |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Cabbage |
| 2 | Green onions | Sliced | Chives | Finish |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Season chicken with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper.
2. Cook chicken in oil in a skillet over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to 165 degrees F, 5 to 7 minutes per side depending on thickness. Rest 5 minutes, then chop or shred.
3. Toss chicken with 1/2 cup BBQ sauce.
4. Toss cabbage with apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, and salt. Set aside for topping after baking.
5. Place flatbreads on sheet pans. Spread each with BBQ sauce, then top with mozzarella, cheddar, BBQ chicken, red onion, and bell pepper.
6. Bake 8 to 12 minutes, until the cheese melts and the flatbread edges are crisp.
7. Top with quick cabbage and green onions. Slice and serve.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Flatbread crispness and whether cabbage topping should stay or be replaced with pickled onions.
- Possible substitutions: Rotisserie chicken can make this a very fast Saturday meal.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Cook and shred chicken Friday or Saturday morning. Assemble flatbreads only right before baking.
- Family preference concerns: Use less BBQ sauce under the cheese if anyone dislikes a sweet pizza-style base.

recipes.title: Chicken Cordon Bleu Skillet with Mashed Potatoes
recipes.markdown:
# Chicken Cordon Bleu Skillet with Mashed Potatoes

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-29/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 35 minutes
- Protein: Chicken breast
- Cuisine or flavor direction: French-inspired chicken, ham, Swiss, creamy Dijon sauce
- Best day to cook: Tuesday
- Perishability notes: Uses chicken, ham, Swiss, cream, and potatoes. Best early-midweek.
- Difficulty: Moderate

## Equipment

- Large skillet
- Medium saucepan or pot
- Potato masher
- Cutting board and knife
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Boneless skinless chicken breasts | Cut into cutlets or chunks | Chicken thighs | Main protein |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Chicken |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Chicken |
| 1 tsp | Garlic powder | Ground | 2 cloves fresh garlic | Chicken |
| 2 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce added salt | Searing |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Searing |
| 8 oz | Deli ham | Thin sliced, chopped | Black forest ham | Skillet |
| 8 oz | Swiss cheese | Sliced or shredded | Gruyere or provolone | Finish |
| 1 cup | Low-sodium chicken broth | Carton | Chicken stock | Sauce |
| 1 cup | Heavy cream | Heavy whipping cream | Half-and-half | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Dijon mustard | Smooth Dijon | Whole grain mustard | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Sauce thickener |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Sauce brightness |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh parsley | Chopped | Dried parsley | Finish |
| 3 lb | Yukon gold potatoes | Peeled or unpeeled | Russet potatoes | Mashed potatoes |
| 4 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce salt | Potatoes |
| 3/4 cup | Milk | Whole or 2 percent | Half-and-half | Potatoes |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Potatoes |

## Basic Instructions

1. Start potatoes: cut potatoes into chunks, cover with cold salted water, and boil until fork-tender, 15 to 20 minutes.
2. Season chicken with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Sear in butter and oil over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to 165 degrees F. Transfer to a plate.
3. Add ham to the skillet and cook 1 to 2 minutes, just to warm and lightly brown.
4. Whisk broth, cream, Dijon, arrowroot, and lemon juice in a bowl. Pour into the skillet and simmer 3 to 5 minutes, scraping browned bits, until lightly thickened.
5. Return chicken to the skillet. Top with Swiss cheese, cover, and cook on low until the cheese melts and the sauce coats the chicken.
6. Drain potatoes and mash with butter, milk, and salt. Add more milk a splash at a time if needed.
7. Serve chicken and ham cream sauce over mashed potatoes. Finish with parsley.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Sauce thickness and whether the family wants more Dijon.
- Possible substitutions: Serve over rice if potatoes feel like too much work.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Cut chicken and ham Tuesday morning. Do not make mashed potatoes ahead for this test.
- Family preference concerns: Swiss can be assertive; provolone is a milder backup.

| 1/2 tsp | Red pepper flakes | Mild | Skip for less heat | Chimichurri |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Chimichurri |
| 1 heart | Romaine lettuce | Chopped | Green leaf lettuce | Salad |
| 1 pint | Grape tomatoes | Halved | Cherry tomatoes | Salad |
| 1/2 | Red onion | Thinly sliced | Shallot | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Lime juice | Salad |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Salad |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Toss potatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 30 to 35 minutes, turning once, until browned and tender.
2. Mix parsley, cilantro, garlic, jalapeno, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, olive oil, oregano, red pepper flakes, and salt. Let chimichurri sit while the steak cooks.
3. Pat steak dry. Rub with oil, salt, and pepper.
4. Grill over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to preferred doneness, about 4 to 6 minutes per side for flank steak. Aim for 130 to 135 degrees F for medium-rare or 140 to 145 degrees F for medium.
5. Rest steak 10 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
6. Toss romaine, tomatoes, and red onion with olive oil, lemon juice, and salt.
7. Serve sliced steak with chimichurri, roasted potatoes, and salad.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Chimichurri acidity and heat level.
- Possible substitutions: Sirloin steak works if flank or skirt is unavailable.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Chop chimichurri herbs Friday morning. Roast potatoes day-of for crisp edges.
- Family preference concerns: Slice against the grain or flank steak can feel chewy.

# Mediterranean Turkey Meatballs with Lemon Rice, Tzatziki, and Roasted Vegetables

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-29/`  
Category: Turkey  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 30 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 35 minutes
- Protein: Ground turkey
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Greek/Mediterranean, lemon, herbs, yogurt sauce
- Best day to cook: Thursday
- Perishability notes: Uses ground turkey, yogurt, cucumber, dill, broccoli, peppers, and zucchini. Best mid-late week.
- Difficulty: Moderate

## Equipment

- Sheet pan
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowls
- Box grater
- Cutting board and knife

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Ground turkey | 93/7 preferred | Ground chicken | Meatballs |
| 1 large | Egg | Large | 2 tbsp Greek yogurt | Binder |
| 1/2 cup | Panko breadcrumbs | Plain | Regular breadcrumbs | Binder |
| 1/3 cup | Feta cheese | Crumbled | Skip if needed | Meatballs |

| 2 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Meatballs and rice |

| 1 tbsp | Fresh dill | Chopped | 1 tsp dried dill | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Dried oregano | Greek oregano preferred | Italian oregano | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Meatballs |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Meatballs |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Lemon rice |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium broth | Lemon rice |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Lemon rice |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Lemon rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Lemon rice |
| 1 1/2 lb | Broccoli florets | Fresh | Frozen broccoli florets | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 | Red bell peppers | Sliced | Orange or yellow peppers | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 | Zucchini | Sliced | Extra bell pepper | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Roasted vegetables |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Roasted vegetables |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Roasted vegetables |
| 1 cup | Plain Greek yogurt | Full-fat or 2 percent | Plain yogurt, strained | Tzatziki |
| 1/2 | English cucumber | Grated and squeezed dry | Persian cucumber | Tzatziki |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh dill | Chopped | 1 tsp dried dill | Tzatziki |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Tzatziki |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Tzatziki |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Toss broccoli, bell peppers, and zucchini with olive oil, salt, and pepper on a sheet pan.
2. Mix turkey, egg, panko, feta, garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, parsley, dill, oregano, salt, and pepper. Shape into 18 to 22 meatballs.
3. Place meatballs on the sheet pan with vegetables or on a second pan. Roast 18 to 22 minutes, until meatballs reach 165 degrees F and vegetables are browned.
4. Cook rice with water, olive oil, and salt. Rest covered 10 minutes, then fluff with lemon juice.
5. Make tzatziki by mixing Greek yogurt, squeezed cucumber, garlic, dill, lemon juice, and salt.
6. Serve meatballs over lemon rice with roasted vegetables and tzatziki.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Turkey meatball moisture. Add an extra spoonful of yogurt if the mixture seems dry.
- Possible substitutions: Ground chicken works, but may be softer to shape.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Make tzatziki Wednesday night. Shape meatballs Thursday morning if cooking that evening.
- Family preference concerns: Keep feta moderate so the meatballs do not turn too salty.

recipes.markdown:
# Orange Beef with Jasmine Rice and Broccoli

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-29/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 25 minutes
- Protein: Beef
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Chinese-American citrus takeout-inspired, orange, ginger, garlic
- Best day to cook: Monday
- Perishability notes: Fresh beef, oranges, and broccoli are best early in the week.
- Difficulty: Easy

## Equipment

- Large skillet or wok
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowls
- Microplane or zester
- Cutting board and knife

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Flank steak | Thinly sliced against the grain | Sirloin or skirt steak | Main protein |
| 1/4 cup | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Coats beef |
| 3 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Searing |
| 1 1/2 lb | Broccoli florets | Fresh | Frozen broccoli florets | Side |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Broccoli |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Broccoli |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Side |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium broth | Rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Rice |
| 1/2 cup | Orange juice | Fresh squeezed | Bottled orange juice | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Orange zest | Fresh | Skip if using bottled juice | Sauce |
| 1/3 cup | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Rice vinegar | Unseasoned | White vinegar | Sauce |
| 3 tbsp | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Honey | Sauce |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Sauce |
| 1/4 tsp | Red pepper flakes | Mild | Skip for less heat | Optional |
| 4 | Green onions | Sliced | Chives | Finish |

## Basic Instructions

1. Start rice: rinse jasmine rice, then simmer with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
2. Whisk orange juice, orange zest, soy sauce, rice vinegar, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes.
3. Toss sliced beef with arrowroot until lightly coated.
4. Stir-fry broccoli in 1 tbsp oil with salt until bright green and crisp-tender, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
5. Sear beef in batches in 3 tbsp oil over medium-high heat. Let the beef brown before turning so the coating gets lightly crisp.
6. Return beef to the skillet, pour in sauce, and simmer 2 to 4 minutes, until glossy and thick enough to coat the beef.
7. Serve orange beef and broccoli over rice. Top with green onions.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Whether the sauce should be sweeter or more orange-forward.
- Possible substitutions: Chicken thighs can work, but cook to 165 degrees F.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Slice beef and broccoli Sunday night. Keep sauce ingredients measured but do not add arrowroot to sauce.
- Family preference concerns: Keep broccoli crisp-tender so the meal still feels quick and fresh.

recipes.markdown:
# Peruvian Chicken with Green Sauce, Roasted Potatoes, and Simple Salad

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-29/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 6
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes, plus marinade time
- Estimated cook time: 45 minutes
- Protein: Chicken
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Peruvian-inspired pollo a la brasa, smoky, garlicky, citrusy, herb sauce
- Best day to cook: Sunday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh chicken, cilantro, jalapeno, lettuce, tomatoes, and yogurt. Best early in the week.
- Difficulty: Signature

## Equipment

- Grill, grill pan, or sheet pan
- Blender or food processor
- Large mixing bowl or zip-top bag
- Sheet pan
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 6 lb | Bone-in chicken leg quarters or thighs | Skin-on | Boneless thighs, reduce cook time | Main protein |
| 3 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Marinade |
| 1/4 cup | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Marinade |
| 8 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 2 tsp garlic powder | Marinade and sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Marinade depth |
| 2 tsp | Ground cumin | Ground | None | Marinade |
| 2 tsp | Smoked paprika | Smoked | Sweet paprika | Marinade |
| 2 tsp | Dried oregano | Dried | 2 tbsp fresh oregano | Marinade |
| 2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Marinade |
| 1 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Marinade |
| 3 lb | Yukon gold potatoes | Cut into wedges | Russet potatoes | Side |
| 2 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Potatoes |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Potatoes |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Potatoes |
| 1 cup | Cilantro | Fresh packed leaves and tender stems | Parsley | Green sauce |
| 1 | Jalapeno | Seeded for mild | Serrano for hotter | Green sauce |
| 1/2 cup | Mayonnaise | Regular | Avocado-oil mayo | Green sauce body |
| 1/2 cup | Plain Greek yogurt | Full-fat or 2 percent | Sour cream | Green sauce tang |
| 2 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Green sauce |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Green sauce |
| 2 hearts | Romaine lettuce | Chopped | Green leaf lettuce | Salad |
| 1 pint | Grape tomatoes | Halved | Cherry tomatoes | Salad |
| 1/2 | Red onion | Thinly sliced | Shallot | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Lemon juice | Salad |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Salad |

## Basic Instructions

1. Mix oil, lime juice, garlic, soy sauce, cumin, smoked paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper. Coat chicken thoroughly and refrigerate at least 2 hours, or up to overnight.
2. Heat grill to medium, or heat oven to 425 degrees F if roasting. Start potatoes first by tossing wedges with oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 35 to 45 minutes, turning once, until browned and tender.
3. Grill chicken skin-side down over medium heat until browned, then move to indirect heat and cook until the thickest pieces reach 165 degrees F. If roasting, place chicken on a sheet pan and roast 40 to 50 minutes.
4. Blend cilantro, jalapeno, mayonnaise, yogurt, garlic, lime juice, and salt until smooth. Taste and adjust lime or salt.
5. Toss romaine, tomatoes, and red onion with olive oil, lime juice, and salt right before serving.
6. Serve chicken with roasted potatoes, salad, and plenty of green sauce.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Green sauce heat level and whether the family prefers yogurt-heavy or mayo-heavy sauce.
- Possible substitutions: Boneless thighs work for faster cooking, but check early and pull at 165 degrees F.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Make marinade and green sauce Sunday morning. Roast potatoes day-of for best texture.
- Family preference concerns: Keep some sauce mild by blending without jalapeno first, then adding jalapeno to the rest.

recipes.markdown:
# Vietnamese Lemongrass Pork Bowls with Pickled Vegetables and Herbs

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-29/`  
Category: Pork  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes, plus quick pickle time
- Estimated cook time: 20 minutes
- Protein: Pork chops
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Vietnamese-inspired lemongrass, fish sauce, lime, herbs
- Best day to cook: Wednesday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh pork, herbs, cucumber, carrots, and radishes. Best midweek.
- Difficulty: Easy

## Equipment

- Large skillet or grill pan
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowls
- Cutting board and knife
- Jar or container for pickles

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Pork chops | Boneless, thin-cut or sliced thin | Pork tenderloin | Main protein |
| 3 stalks | Lemongrass | Tender inner portion minced | 2 tbsp lemongrass paste | Marinade |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Marinade |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Marinade |
| 3 tbsp | Fish sauce | Good-quality | Extra soy sauce | Marinade |
| 2 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Marinade |
| 2 tbsp | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Honey | Marinade |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Marinade |
| 1/2 tsp | Ground coriander | Ground | Skip if unavailable | Marinade |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Marinade |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Bowl base |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium broth | Rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Rice |
| 2 large | Carrots | Cut into matchsticks | Bagged matchstick carrots | Pickles |
| 1 | English cucumber | Thinly sliced | 2 Persian cucumbers | Pickles and bowls |
| 1 bunch | Radishes | Thinly sliced | Extra cucumber | Pickles |
| 1/2 cup | Rice vinegar | Unseasoned | White vinegar | Pickles |
| 2 tbsp | Granulated sugar | White sugar | Honey | Pickles |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Pickles |
| 1/2 cup | Cilantro | Fresh leaves | Parsley | Finish |
| 1/2 cup | Fresh mint | Fresh leaves | Basil | Finish |
| 1/2 cup | Fresh basil | Fresh leaves | Mint | Finish |
| 1 | Lime | Cut into wedges | Bottled lime juice | Serving |

## Basic Instructions

1. Mix lemongrass, garlic, ginger, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, oil, coriander, and black pepper. Coat pork and marinate 20 minutes, or up to 8 hours.
2. Start rice: rinse jasmine rice, then simmer with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
3. Make quick pickles by stirring rice vinegar, sugar, and salt until dissolved. Add carrots, cucumber, and radishes. Let sit at least 20 minutes.
4. Heat a skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat. Cook pork in a thin layer until browned and cooked through, 2 to 4 minutes per side depending on thickness.
5. Slice pork if needed. Build bowls with rice, pork, drained pickled vegetables, herbs, and lime wedges.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Lemongrass strength and pickle sweetness.
- Possible substitutions: Pork tenderloin can be sliced thin and cooked quickly.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Make pickles Tuesday night. Marinate pork Wednesday morning.
- Family preference concerns: Fish sauce smells strong in the bottle but should taste savory and balanced after cooking.

recipes.title: Baked Ziti with Italian Sausage and Garlic Sourdough Bread
recipes.markdown:
# Baked Ziti with Italian Sausage and Garlic Sourdough Bread

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-28/`  
Category: Pasta  
Source or inspiration: Next-week family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 6
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 40 minutes
- Protein: Italian sausage
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Italian-American comfort food, tomato sauce, cheesy pasta
- Best day to cook: Saturday
- Perishability notes: Sausage and dairy should stay cold. This meal has strong leftovers.

## Equipment

- Large pot
- Large skillet
- 9x13 baking dish
- Mixing bowl
- Foil
- Baking sheet

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 lb | Ziti pasta | Dry ziti | Penne or rigatoni | Main pasta |
| 1 1/2 lb | Italian sausage | Mild bulk sausage | Links with casings removed | Main protein |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Neutral oil | If sausage is lean |
| 1 small | Yellow onion | Diced | 1/2 large onion | Sauce |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 32 oz | Marinara sauce | Jarred, good-quality | Homemade tomato sauce | Sauce |
| 14.5 oz | Crushed tomatoes | Canned | Diced tomatoes, crushed by hand | Sauce |
| 1 tsp | Italian seasoning | Dried blend | Dried oregano plus basil | Sauce |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Sauce, adjust to taste |
| 1/4 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Sauce |
| 15 oz | Ricotta cheese | Whole milk | Part-skim | Cheese layer |
| 1 large | Egg | Large | Skip for looser ricotta | Cheese layer |
| 2 cups | Mozzarella cheese | Shredded low-moisture | Italian cheese blend | Topping and filling |
| 1/2 cup | Parmesan cheese | Grated | Pecorino Romano | Divided |
| 2 tbsp | Fresh parsley | Chopped | Basil | Finish |
| 1 loaf | Sourdough bread | Crusty loaf | Italian bread | Garlic bread |
| 6 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted, softened | Salted, reduce added salt | Garlic bread |
| 3 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Garlic bread |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh parsley | Chopped | Dried parsley | Garlic bread |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Garlic bread |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 375 degrees F. Boil ziti in salted water for 2 minutes less than the package says. Drain.
2. Brown sausage in a skillet, breaking it into small pieces. Add olive oil only if the pan is dry.
3. Add onion and cook until softened, 4 to 5 minutes. Add garlic for 30 seconds.
4. Stir in marinara, crushed tomatoes, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Simmer 8 to 10 minutes.
5. Mix ricotta, egg, 1 cup mozzarella, 1/4 cup Parmesan, and parsley in a bowl.
6. Combine pasta with most of the sauce. Layer half the pasta in a 9x13 dish, dollop with ricotta mixture, then add remaining pasta. Top with remaining sauce, 1 cup mozzarella, and 1/4 cup Parmesan.
7. Cover with foil and bake 20 minutes. Uncover and bake 10 to 15 minutes more, until bubbling and browned in spots. Rest 10 minutes before serving.
8. For garlic sourdough, mix softened butter, garlic, parsley, and salt. Spread on sliced sourdough and bake on a sheet pan for 8 to 10 minutes, until toasted.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Whether the family prefers more sauce or more cheese.
- Possible substitutions: Use hot Italian sausage for more heat, or half mild and half hot.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Assemble ziti earlier Saturday and refrigerate. Add 10 to 15 minutes to covered bake time if cold from the fridge.
- Family preference concerns: This is heavier than the rice bowls, so serve with a simple salad next time if desired.

## Review Link

recipes.markdown:
# French Onion Meatballs with Creamy Mashed Potatoes and Green Beans

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-28/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Next-week family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 45 minutes
- Protein: Ground beef
- Cuisine or flavor direction: French onion-style beef gravy, savory, cozy, rich
- Best day to cook: Tuesday
- Perishability notes: Uses ground beef and fresh green beans. Cook by midweek.

## Equipment

- Large deep skillet
- Large pot
- Potato masher
- Sheet pan or plate for shaped meatballs
- Small bowl

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Ground beef | 85/15 or 90/10 | Ground turkey | Meatballs |
| 1 large | Egg | Large | 2 tbsp milk plus 1 tbsp extra panko | Meatball binder |
| 1/2 cup | Panko breadcrumbs | Plain | Regular breadcrumbs | Meatballs |
| 1/4 cup | Milk | Whole or 2 percent | Half-and-half | Meatballs |
| 1 tbsp | Worcestershire sauce | Classic | Soy sauce | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Garlic powder | Ground | 2 cloves fresh minced garlic | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Meatballs |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Meatballs |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Neutral oil | Browning |
| 3 large | Yellow onions | Thinly sliced | Sweet onions | Gravy |
| 3 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce added salt | Gravy |
| 1 tsp | Dried thyme | Dried | 1 tbsp fresh thyme | Gravy |
| 3 cups | Beef broth | Low-sodium | Beef stock | Gravy |
| 1 tbsp | Worcestershire sauce | Classic | Soy sauce | Gravy |
| 2 tbsp | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Gravy thickener |
| 2 tbsp | Cold water | Cold | Broth | Arrowroot slurry |
| 4 oz | Gruyere or Swiss cheese | Shredded | Provolone | Optional topping |
| 2 1/2 lb | Yukon gold potatoes | Peeled or scrubbed | Russet potatoes | Mashed potatoes |
| 5 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce added salt | Mashed potatoes |
| 3/4 cup | Milk | Whole or 2 percent | Half-and-half | Mashed potatoes |
| 1/3 cup | Sour cream | Full-fat | Greek yogurt | Mashed potatoes |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Potatoes, adjust to taste |
| 1 lb | Green beans | Fresh trimmed | Frozen whole green beans | Side |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Butter | Side |
| 1 clove | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1/4 tsp garlic powder | Side |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Side |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Side |

## Basic Instructions

1. Mix beef, egg, panko, milk, Worcestershire, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Shape into 16 to 20 meatballs.
2. Brown meatballs in olive oil in a deep skillet. They do not need to cook through yet. Transfer to a plate.
3. Add butter and onions to the skillet. Cook over medium heat 18 to 25 minutes, stirring often, until onions are deep golden and soft. Add thyme.
4. Add beef broth and Worcestershire. Return meatballs to the skillet, cover, and simmer 12 to 15 minutes, until meatballs reach 160 degrees F.
5. Stir arrowroot with cold water. Add to the simmering gravy and cook 1 to 2 minutes, until glossy and thickened. Add cheese over the meatballs if using, cover briefly, and let melt.
6. Meanwhile, boil potatoes in salted water until fork-tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash with butter, milk, sour cream, and salt.
7. Saute green beans in olive oil until crisp-tender, 6 to 8 minutes. Add garlic for the last minute, then finish with lemon juice and salt.
8. Serve meatballs and gravy over mashed potatoes with green beans on the side.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Whether the onion gravy is rich enough without cheese.
- Possible substitutions: Swiss is more budget-friendly than Gruyere.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Shape meatballs earlier in the day and refrigerate.
- Family preference concerns: Onion texture may matter; cook onions very soft for a smoother gravy feel.

## Basic Instructions

1. Marinate chicken with olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper for 20 minutes or up to 8 hours.
2. Start rice: rinse jasmine rice, then simmer with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
3. Make quick pickled onions by combining sliced red onion, vinegar, sugar, and salt. Let sit at least 20 minutes.
4. Make tzatziki by mixing Greek yogurt, squeezed cucumber, lemon juice, garlic, dill, olive oil, and salt.
5. Cook chicken in a skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat for 5 to 7 minutes per side, until browned and 165 degrees F inside. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
6. Build bowls with rice, chicken, romaine, tomato, cucumber, pickled onion, and tzatziki.

recipes.markdown:
# Jamaican Jerk Chicken with Coconut Rice and Pineapple Mango Salsa

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-28/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Next-week family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 30 minutes, plus 30 minutes marinade time
- Estimated cook time: 35 minutes
- Protein: Chicken thighs
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Jamaican-inspired jerk, smoky, sweet, spicy, citrusy
- Best day to cook: Sunday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh chicken, mango, pineapple, cilantro, lime, and peppers. Best early in the week.

## Equipment

- Grill or grill pan
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowls
- Cutting board and knife
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 1/2 lb | Boneless skinless chicken thighs | Trimmed | Chicken breasts, watched carefully | Main protein |
| 3 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | For marinade and grill |
| 3 tbsp | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Marinade |
| 2 tbsp | Apple cider vinegar | Apple cider vinegar | White vinegar | Marinade tang |
| 2 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | About 1 lime |
| 2 tbsp | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Dark brown sugar | Balances heat |
| 4 | Green onions | Fresh | 1 small shallot | Rough chopped for marinade |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh | 1 tsp garlic powder | Marinade |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Peeled and grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Marinade |
| 1 | Scotch bonnet or habanero pepper | Seeded for less heat | Jalapeno | Start with half if heat-sensitive |
| 2 tsp | Ground allspice | Ground | None | Key jerk flavor |
| 1 tsp | Dried thyme | Dried | 1 tbsp fresh thyme | Marinade |
| 1 tsp | Smoked paprika | Smoked | Sweet paprika | Smoky flavor |
| 1/2 tsp | Ground cinnamon | Ground | None | Marinade |
| 1/4 tsp | Ground nutmeg | Ground | None | Marinade |
| 1/2 tsp | Cayenne pepper | Ground | Red pepper flakes | Optional heat control |
| 1 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Marinade |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Marinade |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Long-grain jasmine | Long-grain white rice | Coconut rice |
| 1 can, 13.5 oz | Coconut milk | Full-fat canned | Light coconut milk | Coconut rice |
| 1 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium chicken broth | Coconut rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Coconut rice |
| 1 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Finish rice |
| 2 cups | Pineapple | Fresh diced | Canned pineapple chunks, drained | Salsa |
| 1 large | Mango | Ripe but firm | Frozen mango, thawed and drained | Salsa |
| 1 | Red bell pepper | Fresh | Orange bell pepper | Salsa |
| 1/4 cup | Red onion | Finely diced | Shallot | Salsa |
| 1/4 cup | Cilantro | Fresh chopped | Parsley | Salsa |
| 1 | Jalapeno | Seeded and minced | Skip for mild salsa | Salsa |
| 1 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Salsa |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Salsa |

## Basic Instructions

1. Blend or finely mash the marinade ingredients: oil, soy sauce, vinegar, lime juice, brown sugar, green onions, garlic, ginger, chile pepper, allspice, thyme, smoked paprika, cinnamon, nutmeg, cayenne, salt, and black pepper.
2. Coat chicken with the marinade. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes, or up to 8 hours.
3. Make the salsa by mixing pineapple, mango, bell pepper, red onion, cilantro, jalapeno, lime juice, and salt. Refrigerate until serving.
4. Rinse rice until the water runs mostly clear. Add rice, coconut milk, water, and salt to a saucepan. Bring to a simmer, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes, then fluff with lime juice.
5. Grill chicken over medium-high heat for 5 to 7 minutes per side, until browned and the thickest pieces reach 165 degrees F. Rest 5 minutes before slicing.
6. Serve sliced jerk chicken over coconut rice with pineapple mango salsa on top.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Family heat tolerance. Start with half a Scotch bonnet or use jalapeno if needed.
- Possible substitutions: Chicken breasts can work but may dry out faster.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Make marinade and salsa Sunday morning. Do not add salsa salt until closer to serving if the fruit is very juicy.
- Family preference concerns: Smoky heat may need a mild portion set aside before adding cayenne or hot pepper.

# Lentil Lettuce Wraps

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-28/`  
Category: Lunches  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe, added for Ally's lunch  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 15 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 25 minutes
- Protein: Red lentils
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Lettuce wraps, soy-ginger, savory
- Best day to cook: Lunch prep
- Perishability notes: Uses butter lettuce, onion, garlic, carrots, and ginger. Lettuce should be used while crisp.

## Equipment

- Saucepan
- Skillet
- Cutting board and knife
- Spoon or spatula

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 cup | Red lentils | Dry red lentils | Brown lentils, longer cook time | Base |
| 2 cups | Broth | Low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth | Water plus salt | Lentils |
| 1 | Yellow onion | Diced | White onion | Filling |
| 3 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Filling |
| 2 | Carrots | Finely diced or shredded | Matchstick carrots | Filling |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Filling |
| 1/4 cup | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Liquid aminos or tamari | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Sesame oil | Toasted preferred | Neutral oil | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Cooking |
| 1 head | Butter lettuce | Whole leaves | Romaine leaves | Serving |

## Basic Instructions

1. Simmer lentils with broth until soft and thick, 12 to 15 minutes.
2. Heat oil in a skillet. Cook onion, carrots, garlic, and ginger until vegetables soften, 4 to 6 minutes.
3. Stir in cooked lentils, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Simmer until thick enough to spoon into lettuce leaves.
4. Cool filling before packing for lunches if not serving immediately.
5. Spoon filling into butter lettuce leaves just before eating.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Whether Ally prefers lentil-only filling or wants an added meat protein in a future version.
- Possible substitutions: Ground beef or ground turkey can be added, but it is not included in this week's grocery list because it is optional.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Cook filling ahead and store separately from lettuce leaves.
- Family preference concerns: Filling needs to be thick, not soupy, or lettuce wraps will be messy.

# Malaysian Peanut Chicken with Jasmine Rice and Stir-Fried Vegetables

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-28/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Next-week family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 30 minutes
- Protein: Chicken thighs
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Malaysian-inspired peanut sauce, savory, nutty, gently spiced
- Best day to cook: Wednesday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh chicken and stir-fry vegetables. Good midweek.

## Equipment

- Large skillet or wok
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowl
- Whisk
- Cutting board and knife

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Boneless skinless chicken thighs | Cut into bite-size pieces | Chicken breast | Main protein |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | For chicken |
| 1/2 cup | Creamy peanut butter | Natural or regular | Crunchy peanut butter | Sauce |
| 1/2 can, about 3/4 cup | Coconut milk | Full-fat canned | Light coconut milk | Sauce |
| 3 tbsp | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fish sauce | Good-quality | Extra soy sauce | Optional but savory |
| 1 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Honey | Keep less sweet than Thai peanut |
| 2 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1/2 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Curry powder | Mild yellow curry powder | Malaysian curry powder | Sauce |
| 1/2 tsp | Ground turmeric | Ground | Skip if unavailable | Color and flavor |
| 1/2 tsp | Ground cumin | Ground | None | Sauce |
| 1/2 tsp | Ground coriander | Ground | None | Sauce |
| 1 tsp | Chili-garlic sauce | Sambal oelek or chili-garlic sauce | Sriracha | Optional heat |
| 1/2 cup | Water | Warm | Chicken broth | Thins sauce |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Side |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold | Low-sodium broth | Rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Rice |
| 2 | Red bell peppers | Sliced | Any bell peppers | Vegetables |
| 8 oz | Snow peas | Fresh | Sugar snap peas | Vegetables |
| 2 large | Carrots | Thinly sliced | Matchstick carrots | Vegetables |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Vegetables |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Vegetables |
| 2 tbsp | Cilantro | Fresh chopped | Green onions | Finish |

## Basic Instructions

1. Start rice: rinse jasmine rice, then simmer with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
2. Whisk peanut butter, coconut milk, soy sauce, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, curry powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, chili-garlic sauce, and warm water until smooth.
3. Cook chicken in oil over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to 165 degrees F, 8 to 10 minutes.
4. Add sauce to chicken and simmer 3 to 5 minutes, stirring often. Add splashes of water if it gets too thick.
5. In a separate skillet or after transferring chicken out briefly, stir-fry bell peppers, snow peas, and carrots in oil with salt until crisp-tender, 4 to 6 minutes.
6. Serve peanut chicken over rice with vegetables and cilantro.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Peanut sauce thickness and salt level. Add water gradually.
- Possible substitutions: Use extra soy sauce instead of fish sauce if needed.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Whisk sauce and cut vegetables the night before.
- Family preference concerns: Keep chili-garlic sauce mild at first.

# Mongolian Beef with Jasmine Rice and Broccoli

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-28/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Next-week family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 25 minutes
- Protein: Beef
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Chinese-American takeout-inspired, savory-sweet, garlicky
- Best day to cook: Monday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh beef and broccoli. Good early-week weeknight meal.

## Equipment

- Large skillet or wok
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowl
- Cutting board and knife
- Tongs

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Flank steak | Thinly sliced against the grain | Sirloin or skirt steak | Main protein |
| 1/4 cup | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Coats beef and thickens sauce |
| 3 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | For searing |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Sauce |
| 1/2 cup | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Sauce |
| 1/2 cup | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium beef broth | Sauce |
| 1/3 cup | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Dark brown sugar | Sauce |
| 1 tsp | Toasted sesame oil | Toasted | Regular sesame oil | Finish |
| 1/4 tsp | Red pepper flakes | Mild | Skip for less heat | Optional |
| 4 | Green onions | Sliced | Chives | Finish |
| 1 1/2 lb | Broccoli | Fresh florets | Frozen broccoli florets | Side |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | For broccoli |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Broccoli |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Side |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium broth | Rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Rice |

## Basic Instructions

1. Start rice: rinse jasmine rice, then simmer with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
2. Toss sliced beef with arrowroot until lightly coated.
3. Whisk soy sauce, water, brown sugar, sesame oil, and red pepper flakes in a bowl.
4. Stir-fry broccoli in 1 tbsp oil with salt until bright green and crisp-tender, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
5. Sear beef in batches in 3 tbsp oil over medium-high heat. Let pieces brown before turning so the coating gets lightly crisp.
6. Add garlic and ginger for 30 seconds. Pour in sauce and simmer 2 to 4 minutes, until glossy and thick enough to coat the beef.
7. Serve beef and broccoli over rice. Top with green onions.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Sauce sweetness. Reduce brown sugar to 1/4 cup if the family wants it less sweet.
- Possible substitutions: Chicken can be used, but cook to 165 degrees F.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Slice beef and broccoli the night before. Keep beef uncovered or loosely covered in the fridge for easier searing.
- Family preference concerns: Broccoli texture matters; keep it crisp-tender, not soft.

recipes.title: Steakhouse Garlic Butter Steak Bites with Roasted Vegetables and Crispy Baby Potatoes
recipes.markdown:
# Steakhouse Garlic Butter Steak Bites with Roasted Vegetables and Crispy Baby Potatoes

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `weekly-plans/2026/week-28/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Next-week family menu idea inspired by liked steak bites  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 40 minutes
- Protein: Steak
- Cuisine or flavor direction: American steakhouse, garlic butter, roasted vegetables
- Best day to cook: Friday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh steak, mushrooms, zucchini, and summer squash. Cook before the weekend if bought early.

## Equipment

- Large cast-iron skillet or heavy skillet
- Sheet pan
- Large pot
- Mixing bowls
- Cutting board and knife

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Sirloin steak | Cut into 1-inch bites | New York strip or ribeye | Main protein |
| 1 1/2 lb | Baby potatoes | Halved | Yukon gold potatoes, cubed | Crispy potatoes |
| 12 oz | Mushrooms | Cremini or baby bella, halved | White mushrooms | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 medium | Zucchini | Cut into half-moons | Extra summer squash | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 medium | Summer squash | Cut into half-moons | Extra zucchini | Roasted vegetables |
| 4 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Potatoes and vegetables |
| 1 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Divided |
| 1 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Divided |
| 1 tsp | Steak seasoning | Low-salt preferred | Montreal-style seasoning | Steak |
| 1 tbsp | Worcestershire sauce | Classic | Soy sauce | Steak |
| 4 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce added salt | Garlic butter |
| 5 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 1/2 tsp garlic powder | Garlic butter |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh parsley | Chopped | Chives | Finish |
| 1 tsp | Dried thyme | Dried | 1 tbsp fresh thyme | Vegetables |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Searing steak |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Boil halved baby potatoes in salted water for 8 to 10 minutes, until just tender. Drain well.
2. Toss potatoes with 2 tbsp olive oil, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper. Roast cut-side down for 25 to 30 minutes, flipping once, until crisp and browned.
3. Toss mushrooms, zucchini, and summer squash with 2 tbsp olive oil, thyme, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper. Roast on a separate area or second sheet pan for 18 to 22 minutes, until browned at the edges.
4. Pat steak bites very dry. Season with steak seasoning, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper, and Worcestershire.
5. Heat neutral oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Sear steak bites in batches 2 to 3 minutes per side, until browned. Avoid crowding.
6. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add butter and garlic to the skillet, then return steak bites and toss 30 to 60 seconds until coated. Pull steak around 130 to 135 degrees F for medium-rare or 140 to 145 degrees F for medium.
7. Finish steak with parsley and serve with crispy potatoes and roasted vegetables.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Preferred steak doneness and whether potatoes need more crisping time.
- Possible substitutions: Use pre-cut steak tips if they look fresh and evenly sized.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Cut vegetables earlier Friday and refrigerate dry. Do not cut potatoes too early unless stored in cold water and dried very well.
- Family preference concerns: Keep garlic butter separate for anyone who wants lighter sauce.

## Planning Summary

- Estimated cook time: 2 1/2 to 3 1/2 hours
- Protein: Pork roast
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Classic roast dinner, garlic, rosemary, thyme
- Best day to cook: Sunday or weekend
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh pork roast and root vegetables. Cook pork within 2 days of shopping.

## Equipment

- Dutch oven or roasting pan with lid or foil
- Cutting board and knife
- Tongs
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 3 to 4 lb | Pork roast | Pork shoulder or pork loin roast | Pork butt | Main protein |
| 2 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Browning |
| 2 lb | Potatoes | Yukon gold, chunked | Russet potatoes | Vegetable |
| 1 lb | Carrots | Peeled and chunked | Baby carrots | Vegetable |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 325 degrees F. Pat pork dry and season with salt, pepper, rosemary, and thyme.
2. Heat oil in a Dutch oven and brown pork on all sides, 8 to 10 minutes total.
3. Add potatoes, carrots, onion, garlic, and chicken broth around the pork.
4. Cover and roast until tender. Pork shoulder may need 3 to 3 1/2 hours; pork loin may need 1 1/2 to 2 hours and should be pulled around 145 degrees F.
5. Rest pork 10 to 15 minutes. Slice or shred depending on cut.
6. Serve pork with vegetables and pan juices.

Weekly folder: `recipe-archive/drafts/`  
Category: Lunches  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 15 minutes if cooking chicken
- Protein: Grilled chicken and hard boiled eggs
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Protein-forward chopped salad, vinaigrette
- Best day to cook: Lunch prep or light dinner

- Mixing bowl
- Cutting board and knife

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |

archivedRecipes.markdown:
# Steak Bites with Roasted Vegetables

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `recipe-archive/drafts/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 30 minutes
- Protein: Steak
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Steakhouse-style, garlic, paprika, roasted vegetables
- Best day to cook: Midweek
- Perishability notes: Uses steak, Brussels sprouts, zucchini, squash, and mushrooms. Cook before vegetables soften.

## Equipment

- Cast iron skillet or large heavy skillet
- Baking sheet
- Mixing bowl
- Cutting board and knife
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Steak | Sirloin, cut into 1-inch bites | New York strip or ribeye | Main protein |
| 12 oz | Brussels sprouts | Trimmed and halved | Broccoli florets | Vegetable |
| 2 | Zucchini | Sliced thick | Extra squash | Vegetable |
| 2 | Yellow squash | Sliced thick | Extra zucchini | Vegetable |
| 12 oz | Mushrooms | Cremini or baby bella | White mushrooms | Vegetable |
| 3 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Vegetables and steak |
| 1 tsp | Garlic powder | Ground | Fresh garlic added at end | Seasoning |
| 1 tsp | Onion powder | Ground | Skip if unavailable | Seasoning |
| 1 tsp | Paprika | Smoked or sweet | Smoked paprika | Seasoning |
| 1 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Divided |
| 1 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Divided |
| 1 tsp | Italian seasoning | Dried blend | Oregano plus basil | Vegetables |
| 2 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce added salt | Finish |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Toss Brussels sprouts, zucchini, squash, and mushrooms with 2 tbsp olive oil, Italian seasoning, half the salt, and half the pepper.
2. Roast vegetables 22 to 28 minutes, stirring once, until browned and tender.
3. Pat steak bites dry. Season with garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, remaining salt, and remaining pepper.
4. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. Sear steak in batches so pieces brown instead of steam.
5. Add butter at the end and toss steak bites until glossy. Pull steak around 130 to 135 degrees F for medium-rare or 140 to 145 degrees F for medium.
6. Serve steak bites with roasted vegetables.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Steak doneness and vegetable mix.
- Possible substitutions: Baby potatoes can be added if a starch is needed.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Cut vegetables earlier in the day; cut steak just before cooking if possible.
- Family preference concerns: Avoid crowding the skillet or the steak will not brown well.

# Thai Peanut Stir Fry

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Weekly folder: `recipe-archive/drafts/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 20 minutes
- Protein: Chicken or tofu
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Thai-inspired peanut sauce, savory-sweet, ginger-garlic
- Best day to cook: Midweek
- Perishability notes: Uses chicken or tofu plus broccoli, peppers, and carrots. Cook fresh vegetables before they soften.

## Equipment

- Wok or large skillet
- Mixing bowl
- Cutting board and knife
- Spoon or spatula

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Chicken breast or tofu | Chicken sliced thin, extra-firm tofu pressed | Chicken thighs | Protein |
| 2 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Stir fry |
| 1 lb | Broccoli florets | Fresh | Frozen broccoli florets | Vegetable |
| 2 | Bell peppers | Red, yellow, or orange, sliced | Extra broccoli | Vegetable |
| 2 | Carrots | Thinly sliced | Matchstick carrots | Vegetable |
| 1/2 cup | Peanut butter | Creamy | Crunchy peanut butter | Sauce |
| 1/4 cup | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Honey | Liquid honey | Brown sugar | Sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Rice vinegar | Unseasoned | Lime juice | Sauce |
| 3 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Sesame oil | Toasted preferred | Neutral oil | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Slurry |
| 1/2 cup | Water | Warm | Coconut milk | Sauce thinning |
| 1 1/2 cups dry | Jasmine rice | Long-grain jasmine | Long-grain white rice | Serving |

## Basic Instructions

1. Cook rice according to package directions if serving with rice.
2. Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, arrowroot, and warm water until smooth.
3. Heat oil in a wok. Cook chicken until browned and 165 degrees F, or cook tofu until golden. Transfer to a plate.
4. Stir fry broccoli, bell peppers, and carrots until crisp-tender.
5. Return protein to the pan. Add peanut sauce and simmer 2 to 4 minutes, until thickened and coating everything.
6. Serve over rice.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Sauce thickness and peanut intensity.
- Possible substitutions: Coconut milk can replace part of the water for a richer sauce.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Make sauce earlier the same day and refrigerate.
- Family preference concerns: Peanut sauce thickens quickly; loosen with water if it gets pasty.

planningDocs.title: Grocery Rules
planningDocs.markdown:
# Grocery Rules

## Non-Negotiables

- Grocery lists are recipe-driven only.
- Assume nothing is on hand.
- Do not add household staples unless required by a recipe.
- Every grocery item must list quantity, preferred version/type, acceptable alternatives, and recipe ownership.
- Use arrowroot instead of cornstarch unless the recipe specifically requires cornstarch.

## Required Item Format

| Quantity | Item | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Recipe |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Chicken thighs | Boneless skinless | Chicken breast if adjusted for cook time | Example recipe |

## Quality Checks

- Can someone shop from the list without asking follow-up questions?
- Does every item belong to a recipe?
- Are perishables tied to the week schedule?
- Are proteins specified by cut, weight, and condition such as fresh, frozen, bone-in, or boneless?

planningDocs.title: Prep Rules
planningDocs.markdown:
# Prep Rules

Prep should make the week easier without damaging texture, freshness, or food safety.

## Required Sections

- Sunday Dinner Support
- Future Meal Prep
- Protein Thaw Schedule
- Wednesday Refresh
- Do Not Prep Ahead

## Task Requirements

Every prep task must include:

- Ingredients
- Instructions
- Storage method
- Use-by date
- Meal ownership

## Good Prep Candidates

- Wash and dry sturdy produce.
- Mix dry spice blends.
- Portion sauces that hold well.
- Thaw proteins on a clear schedule.
- Cook grains only when texture will hold for the planned meal.

## Do Not Prep Ahead Without a Reason

- Delicate herbs
- Crispy toppings
- Seafood marinades
- Cut avocado
- Anything that will make a meal worse by Wednesday
